reviews.comment:
Antes el Shop era bueno, tenías de todo. Sandwiches elaborados, pebetes varios, empanadas caseras varias y riquísimas, menú del día al plato, toda clase de golosinas y cigarrillos, un buen servicio de cafetería etc. Era "EL LUGAR".
Luego cambiaron de dueño, y se fueron a pique. Ya ni siquiera tienen sandwiches de miga clásicos envasados, y los que tienen van por docena y te los cobran más caras que una grande de muzza en Las Cuartetas. Las empanadas se extinguieron. Ir a comprar cigarrillos, o galletitas, o unas medialunas del dia es algo que quedó en el olvido. Para colmo, vas a comprarte un Guaymallén (y en el eventual caso de que tengan) te sale lo mismo que un Milka, Terrabusi, Cachafaz, o la marca que quieras.
Se pusieron carísimos en comparación a otras estaciones de servicio de la zona, y además se quedaron en el tiempo con las instalaciones.
Vayas a comprar lo que vayas a comprar, andá sabiendo que no lo tienen; hoy entrar en el Shop, es salir con las manos vacías....Y si terminás comprando algo, te lo cobran al doble del valor del dolar blue.

La atención es algo que también se vino para abajo. Antes eran atentos y serviciales todas y cada una de las personas que ahí trabajan. Hoy, si te vas a pedir un café (bah, café....agua caliente teñida e insípida, ya ni eso vale la pena), podés estar esperando horas. Y no porque no den abasto, sino porque no se preocupan demasiado....están mas pendientes del celular o de la charla con un colega, que en atender a un cliente.
Pasó de ser una de las mejores, a ser de las peores de la zona en cuestión de escaso tiempo.  Una lástima realmente.

(Translated by Google)
The Shop used to be good; you had everything. Elaborate sandwiches, various sandwiches, a variety of delicious homemade empanadas, a daily menu, all kinds of sweets and cigarettes, good cafeteria service, etc. It was "THE PLACE."
Then they changed owners, and they went under. They don't even have classic packaged sandwiches anymore, and the ones they do have are sold by the dozen and they charge more than a large mozzarella sandwich at Las Cuartetas. The empanadas are extinct. Going to buy cigarettes, or cookies, or some croissants of the day is something that's been forgotten. To top it all off, if you buy a Guaymallén (and if they have one), it costs the same as a Milka, Terrabusi, Cachafaz, or whatever brand you want.
They became incredibly expensive compared to other gas stations in the area, and they also lost their facilities over time.
Whatever you're going to buy, know they don't have it. Today, entering the Shop means leaving empty-handed... And if you end up buying something, they charge you double the value of the blue dollar.

Customer service is also something that's gone downhill. Before, every single person working there was attentive and helpful. Today, if you go to order a coffee (well, coffee... hot water with a touch of color and tastelessness, not even that's worth it anymore), you can wait for hours. And not because they can't keep up, but because they don't care enough... They're more focused on their cell phones or chatting with a colleague than serving a customer.
It went from being one of the best to one of the worst in the area in a short amount of time. A real shame.
reviews.name: accounts/109853746269412609224/locations/12305613765678893423/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUR4aEpURUN3EAE
